Title: Bamana Ci Wara-Style Antelope Headdress
Description: Bamana-style Ci Wara antelope headdress carved in wood with arched openwork body, long horns, and abstracted antelope features. Ci Wara headdresses are associated in public museum records with Bamana agricultural masquerade traditions honoring the mythical being who taught cultivation; exact local workshop, pair relationship, and performance history are not documented.

The angular planes and mask form closely match Lwalwa/Lwalu mask records, though exact local name and performance context are not independently confirmed for this object.
Subject: initiation rites; funerary ceremonies; ancestral veneration
Geographic location: Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kasai region or southern DRC/Angola border region
Publisher: Jackson State University. (electronic version); JSUMS ART. (electronic version)

Exact local name and use remain undocumented.
